source_citation: Dayan et al. 2010 Biochim Biophys Acta 1804:1548-1556 (kinetics + MD); mutation orig. Patzoldt et al. 2006 PNAS 103:12329-12334
notes: Accessions used directly in Dayan 2010's own Methods section - primary pair for the validation gate. Alternative account (Hao et al. 2009, J Phys Chem B 113:4865-4875, DOI 10.1021/jp807442n): loss of interchain Gly210-Ser424 H-bond - CONFIRMED directly from Hao 2009's own abstract (not just Dayan's summary of it). Weight Dayan 2010 as primary per DECISION_LOG; Hao 2009 is purely computational, no wet-lab kinetics.

source_citation: Patzoldt et al. 2006 PNAS 103:12329-12334; same mechanism as pair1 per Dayan 2010
notes: Second biological replicate pair from original Patzoldt 2006 deposit; not a duplicate/conflict with pair1. Background diffs vs pair1 WT: S476C (pair2 only) not implicated in resistance.

Resistant allele carries 3 substitutions (S68N, V361A, R480T); paper's site-directed mutagenesis showed V361A alone is causal, R480T alone is proven non-causal, S68N is background variation. Mechanism class not yet resolved from a structural paper - flagged open, do not infer.

source_citation: Rangani et al. 2019 Front Plant Sci 10:568
notes: R1 resistant genotype carries G399A plus background polymorphisms (E38D S68N R377G K432N) relative to S3 susceptible reference - none of the background positions implicated as causal.

source_citation: Rangani et al. 2019 Front Plant Sci 10:568
notes: R2 resistant genotype: clean single-position difference from S3 at position 399 (G->A) - cleanest confirmation of G399A in this dataset.

source_citation: Giacomini et al. 2017 Pest Manag Sci 73:1559-1563
notes: OPEN ITEM, CONFIRMED via full-text read (not just visible-text search): no GenBank accessions appear anywhere in Giacomini et al. 2017, including References/Acknowledgements. Sequencing was cDNA-based on individual plants (populations GPW/LPW/SPW resistant, KPW sensitive; Table 1 gives per-plant genotypes), not deposited full-length sequences. R98L (a different substitution at the homologous site) belongs to Ambrosia artemisiifolia (common ragweed), confirmed verbatim in the paper's own text. Mechanism detail (H-bond disruption with acifluorfen/fomesafen/sulfentrazone) still cited from Hao et al. 2014 JAFC, not yet read directly. Do not resolve accessions by inference; needs supplementary data or author correspondence.

source_citation: Larran et al. 2017, Pest Manag Sci, Table 4 (allelic version C, R1 population, 1/8 clones)
notes: Confirmed directly from paper's own Table 4 - S population (KY781916) explicitly labeled wild-type/susceptible, 8/8 clones identical. Native numbering = Arabidopsis thaliana convention per paper's own text, and matches the 1Z8N crystal structure's own residue numbering exactly (verified: residue 574 in 1Z8N chain A is TRP). No offset/alignment work needed, unlike PPO. Previously-reported TSR substitution (bold in Table 4) - well-established in the ALS literature (Tranel & Wright 2002 review, cited as ref 36).

source_citation: Larran et al. 2017, Pest Manag Sci, Table 4 (allelic version H, R2 population, 2/8 clones)
notes: Same wild-type reference as Trp574Leu. Two independent clones (both accession KY781923.1 after dedup - Table 4 lists frequency 2/8) carry this single, isolated substitution. Verified: residue 653 in 1Z8N chain A is SER. Previously-reported TSR substitution (bold in Table 4).

source_citation: Larran et al. 2017, Pest Manag Sci, Table 4 (allelic version E, R2 population, 2/8 clones); position 122 is an established ALS TSR site (Tranel & Wright 2002)
notes: Larran 2017 reports A122S in A. palmeri (first plant report of Ser at 122; position 122 is a well-established ALS TSR site). CAVEAT: in Larran's deposits A122S co-occurs with A282D (allelic version E / KY781920), so A122S individual causality was not isolated; confidence=medium. Structure mapping: 1Z8N chain A residue 122 is ALA (verified), Arabidopsis-convention numbering, in the dimer-interface active-site core. Additional clean single-allele A. palmeri ALS positions (Pro197, Asp376) require the paywalled Kupper et al. 2018 Pest Manag Sci 74:x (DOI 10.1002/ps.5232) - see HANDOFF_NEXT_STEPS.md.

source_citation: Singh et al. 2018, Pest Manag Sci 74:2286-2295 (Pro197Ala detected in A. palmeri, MIS11-A); Ji et al. 2025, Plants 14:525 (Pro197 in A. palmeri); position 197 is a well-established ALS TSR site (Tranel & Wright 2002)
notes: Singh 2018 detected Pro197Ala in A. palmeri (found together with Trp574Leu; Pro197Ala confers SU resistance per the paper). Reference-numbered to the A. palmeri ALS reference KT833339.1 (susceptible); Singh deposited no per-plant mutant accessions (their 'accessions' are field localities, not GenBank deposits), so no paired mutant accession is claimed - same treatment as the ACCase reference-numbered rows. Pro197 is one of the most firmly established ALS TSR positions across weeds. Structure mapping: 1Z8N chain A residue 197 is PRO (verified), Arabidopsis-convention numbering, in the dimer-interface active-site core. Asp376 was NOT added: Singh 2018 lists it only as a known locus and did not detect it in their A. palmeri plants, and no A. palmeri Asp376 primary source is in hand.

source_citation: Baerson et al. 2002 Plant Physiology 129:1265-1275; PMID 12114580
notes: Peer-reviewed GenBank-linked accession pair: PubMed metadata lists AJ417033/AJ417034; GenBank records label AJ417033 as epsps-R and AJ417034 as epsps-S. Baerson reports mature-protein Pro106Ser; GenBank translated partial-protein difference is position 107 (resistant=Ser, susceptible=Pro), mapped by local alignment to 8UMJ maize sequence position 107 / PDB residue 106. Correction note: 8UMJ includes an initiating MET numbered 0, so FASTA sequence position 107 corresponds to PDB residue 106, not 108. AJ417033 also carries a second translated difference at position 382 (resistant=Leu, susceptible=Pro), but Baerson reports that the second substitution does not contribute significantly to reduced glyphosate sensitivity.

source_citation: Delye et al. 2005 Plant Physiology 137:794-806; Yu et al. 2007 Plant Physiology 145:547-558
notes: Reference/numbering accession verified from Delye 2005 text: all nucleotide and amino-acid positions correspond to black-grass chloroplastic ACCase AJ310767. Mutation is a previously established ACCase resistance substitution in Delye 2005 and is detected in resistant Lolium populations in Yu 2007 Table III. Structure mapping uses AJ310767 CT-domain alignment to 1UYS; Delye 2005 assigns the 1781 side chain to the C-chain side of the dimeric binding cavity.

source_citation: Delye et al. 2005 Plant Physiology 137:794-806; Yu et al. 2007 Plant Physiology 145:547-558
notes: Delye 2005 identifies Trp2027Cys from sequencing resistant black-grass seedlings and concludes it confers APP resistance but not CHD resistance; Yu 2007 detects 2027-Cys in resistant Lolium plants. No single full-length resistant accession is claimed; row is reference-numbered to AJ310767.

source_citation: Delye et al. 2005 Plant Physiology 137:794-806; Yu et al. 2007 Plant Physiology 145:547-558
notes: Previously established ACCase resistance substitution discussed in Delye 2005 using AJ310767 numbering; Yu 2007 detects 2041-Asn in resistant Lolium plants. Structure mapping uses B-chain homolog in 1UYS.

source_citation: Delye et al. 2005 Plant Physiology 137:794-806; Yu et al. 2007 Plant Physiology 145:547-558
notes: Delye 2005 identifies Asp2078Gly from resistant black-grass seedlings and concludes it confers resistance to APPs and CHDs; Yu 2007 detects 2078-Gly broadly in resistant Lolium populations. No single full-length resistant accession is claimed; row is reference-numbered to AJ310767.

Yu 2007 identifies Cys2088Arg as a new ACCase resistance mutation in resistant Lolium populations, states that amino-acid positions correspond to full-length plastidic ACCase in A. myosuroides, and deposits the 2088-region sequences as EF538937-EF538943. The assembled Lolium contig is closest to plastidic Lolium ACCase AY995232 and maps to AJ310767 numbering for structural comparison.

Delye 2005 identifies Gly2096Ala from resistant black-grass seedlings and concludes it confers APP resistance but not CHD resistance. Row is reference-numbered to AJ310767; no single full-length resistant accession is claimed.
